chunk_summary: Guidance for Indian families on portion control, food swaps, and label reading to manage metabolic risk in children, emphasizing practical strategies like hand-based portioning, culturally acceptable swaps, and sugar avoidance in snacks and beverages.
learning_objectives: Understand hand-based portion guidance for managing metabolic risk in children.
Learn culturally appropriate food swaps to reduce refined starch and sugar intake.
Develop skills for reading food labels to identify hidden sugars and unhealthy ingredients.
Implement strategies to manage school snack and beverage choices for children.
key_facts: Portion sizes: Protein (1 palm), starch (1 closed fist or half-fist for high triglycerides), non-starchy vegetables (2 fists), added fats (1 thumb).
Replace one serving of white rice or roti with dal, rajma, chana, or soy plus vegetables to increase fiber and protein.
Desserts should be replaced with fruit and nuts on at least 5 days per week; sweets reserved for planned occasions.
Avoid snacks with sugar, jaggery syrup, glucose, or maltodextrin listed in the first three ingredients.
Strict beverage rule: Sweet-tasting drinks are treated as sugar unless plain milk, unsweetened chaas, or water.
School snacks should be pre-planned, with canteen purchases treated as occasional.
reference_quote: Portion guidance should be practical enough for busy households and should adapt to the triglyceride-high phenotype. A hand-based guide avoids weighing food. (Source: IAP guidelines on childhood obesity, 2023)
